description: First-generation exploratory mechanistic interpretability (Qwen2.5-Math-7B, 4-bit NF4 on a T4) in Jupyter notebooks: MLP/attention and per-neuron DLA, *-token activation patching, path-DLA (L27H11 discovery), the L22 causal hub, +-token context-independence, and L27H11 enforcer-vs-amplifier tests (K1-K3: context-specific DLA, head-output cosine, head-output patching). Related: l27h11_composition_experiments.ipynb, plus_star_interaction.ipynb.
motivation: Localize and characterize the precedence circuit in a single model before scaling.
formal_results: Narrated findings (notebook outputs are stripped - no saved numbers): L27H11 = strongest *->= routing head; L22 = causal hub; + token context-independent; ~40% bimodal circuit engagement; L27H11 reads as an amplifier (high head-output cosine across contexts). This is the 4-bit single-layer/single-model framing.
results_description: An initial single-layer story (L27H11 router + L22 lookup hub); outputs are not saved in the notebooks.
conclusions: A first-pass circuit, later found to be partly an artifact of 4-bit quantization and single-layer summaries (reframed by e-0003/e-0004).

comments.superseded: Notebooks have outputs stripped (methods/markdown only). The 4-bit single-layer framing (L27H11 dominant router, L22 lookup hub) is SUPERSEDED by the bf16 multi-model sweep (e-0003/e-0004), which refutes it.
